Legal Disclaimer and Confidentiality Notice 
This Request for Proposal (RFP) document (Reference No.: AILL-PROC-2025-0078) is issued by Apex 
Industrial Logistics Limited, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013 and registered in India, 
with its registered office at Plot 14, Logistics Park, Bhiwandi, Maharashtra — 421 302, India (hereinafter 
referred to as "Apex" or "the Issuing Authority").
This document is issued solely for the purpose of soliciting proposals from qualified Managed Service 
Providers (MSPs) and System Integrators for the managed network transformation programme described 
herein. It does not constitute a commitment, representation, or warranty by Apex to enter into any contractual 
relationship. Apex reserves the absolute right, at its sole discretion, to:
• 
Accept or reject any or all proposals received in response to this RFP, without assigning any reason 
and without incurring any liability to any responding vendor;
• 
Cancel, modify, or re-issue this RFP at any time without prior notice;
• 
Waive any informality or irregularity in a proposal;
• 
Negotiate with one or more vendors simultaneously;
• 
Award a contract to a vendor whose proposal is not the lowest-cost submission;
• 
Conduct due diligence verification on all information submitted by responding vendors.
All information, data, network topology details, specifications, and documentation contained in this RFP are 
the proprietary intellectual property of Apex and are provided in strict confidence under the Information 
Technology Act, 2000 and the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023. By accepting and retaining this RFP, 
the recipient acknowledges and agrees to be bound by these confidentiality obligations.
NOTE: The submission of a proposal in response to this RFP does not entitle any vendor to 
reimbursement of costs incurred in the preparation of said proposal. All proposal preparation 
costs are borne exclusively by the responding vendor.

SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction
1.1  About Apex Industrial Logistics Limited 
Apex Industrial Logistics Limited (hereinafter "Apex" or "the Company") is one of India's leading integrated 
logistics and heavy supply chain operators. Founded in 1994 and headquartered in Bhiwandi, Maharashtra, 
Apex operates a national network of three hundred (300) branch warehouses spanning twenty-eight (28) Indian 
states and Union Territories, with a centralised Tier-III Data Centre located in Andheri East, Mumbai.
Apex employs approximately 15,000 personnel across its operational footprint and recorded consolidated 
annual revenue of approximately INR 5,500 Crore in Financial Year 2024–25. The Company's core business 
verticals encompass bulk freight forwarding, automated warehousing, cold-chain logistics, last-mile 
distribution, and cross-border trade facilitation. Apex is a publicly listed entity on the National Stock Exchange 
(NSE: APEXIL) and is subject to SEBI listing obligations.
1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers 
Apex's current enterprise network infrastructure was architected over a decade ago and is now a material 
constraint on operational agility, application performance, and security posture. The incumbent environment 
presents the following critical and unacceptable constraints:
Pain Point
Current Impact on Operations
Legacy MPLS Network
All 300 branch warehouses are connected via legacy Multiprotocol Label Switching 
(MPLS) circuits procured from a single incumbent carrier. Bandwidth tiers are rigid 
(2 Mbps to 10 Mbps per branch), costly to upgrade, and require 60–90-day lead 
times for any configuration change.
Chronic Network 
Outages
The Company experienced an average of 4.3 unplanned network outages per 
month per region in FY2024–25, each lasting an average of 47 minutes. These 
outages directly halt warehouse management system (WMS) operations, fleet 
tracking updates, and ERP transaction processing, resulting in an estimated 
revenue impact of INR 3.8 Crore per month.
Single-Link 
Dependency
Hub warehouse sites (Tier-1) currently operate on a single MPLS link with no 
active failover path. Loss of connectivity at a hub site cascades to 8–15 dependent 
spoke sites reliant on the hub for WMS access and IoT aggregation.
No Cloud On-Ramp
Apex is executing a cloud-first transformation programme (ERP on Microsoft 
Azure, data analytics on AWS). Currently, all cloud traffic is backhauled through 
the Mumbai Data Centre, introducing latency of 180–340 ms for branch-to-cloud 
workloads and consuming disproportionate Data Centre bandwidth.
Security Perimeter 
Gaps
Branch sites lack next-generation firewall (NGFW) protection. A significant 
ransomware incident in Q3 FY2024 resulted in operational downtime of 72 hours 
across 14 warehouse sites and a forensic investigation cost of INR 1.1 Crore. Apex 
currently has no 24/7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) capability.
IoT Connectivity 
Fragmentation
Apex operates a fleet of 2.5 million IoT-enabled tracking SIMs across its vehicle 
fleet and warehouse assets. SIM management is currently distributed across three 
different Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs), resulting in fragmented 
visibility, inconsistent data plans, and inability to enforce unified MDM policies.
1.3  Programme Objectives 
Apex Industrial Logistics Limited hereby issues this Request for Proposal to solicit proposals from qualified 
Managed Service Providers and System Integrators for the complete overhaul of its enterprise network, cloud 
connectivity, and security infrastructure. The primary objectives of this programme are:
• 
Decommission all legacy MPLS circuits across 300 branch sites and replace with a fully managed 
SD-WAN overlay with active-active dual-link connectivity at hub sites.
• 
Establish direct, carrier-grade Cloud Interconnects to Microsoft Azure (primary) and Amazon Web 
Services (secondary) from the Mumbai Data Centre, eliminating cloud backhaul latency.

• 
Deploy a 24/7 Managed Security Operations Centre (SOC) with comprehensive monitoring of all 
branch-edge next-generation firewalls, threat intelligence feeds, and incident response capability.
• 
Consolidate all 2.5 million fleet and asset IoT SIMs onto a single Enterprise Mobility platform with 
unified management, real-time visibility, and enforced MDM policy.
• 
Achieve a minimum of 99.99% network availability (Four Nines) at all Tier-1 hub warehouse sites on 
a monthly measurement basis.
• 
Deliver the complete programme within 18 months of contract execution, migrating all 300 sites 
across five defined phases.
• 
Ensure full compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act) for all data 
processed on or transmitted through the new network infrastructure.

2.2  Technology Environment 
Apex's current enterprise technology stack is as follows, provided for the purposes of integration planning:
• 
ERP Platform: SAP S/4HANA (hosted on Microsoft Azure — Central India region), migrated in 
FY2023.
• 
Warehouse Management System (WMS): Manhattan Associates WMS — on-premises at Mumbai 
DC, with active Azure DR replication.
• 
Fleet & Asset Tracking: Proprietary IoT platform with 2.5 million active SIMs; data aggregation via 
MQTT brokers hosted on AWS (Mumbai region).
• 
Collaboration: Microsoft 365 (Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint) — Microsoft Azure hosted.
• 
IT Service Management: ServiceNow (ITSM) — cloud-hosted; ITIL v4 aligned.
• 
Identity: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) with on-premises Active Directory synchronisation 
via Entra Connect.
• 
Security: Checkpoint firewalls at Mumbai DC (end-of-support 2026); no unified SIEM; no SOC.
• 
Network: Tata Communications MPLS (primary); no SD-WAN; no cloud interconnect.
2.3  Regulatory and Compliance Context 
Apex's network and data infrastructure is subject to the following regulatory frameworks, all of which must be 
addressed in vendor proposals:
• 
Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act): All personal data of employees, customers, 
and fleet drivers processed across the network must comply with DPDP Act provisions including 
data localisation, consent management, and data principal rights.
• 
SEBI Cybersecurity and Cyber Resilience Framework (CSCRF) 2024: As a listed entity, Apex is 
subject to SEBI's CSCRF, mandating 24x7 SOC capability, annual VAPT, and Board-level 
cybersecurity governance.
• 
Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Payment Security Guidelines: Applicable to logistics payment and 
settlement flows processed through the network.
• 
ISO/IEC 27001:2022: Apex holds certification and requires all MSP subcontractors to maintain 
equivalent or superior certification.

SECTION 3 — Project Overview and Objectives
3.1  Programme Description 
The Apex Network Transformation Programme (hereinafter "the Programme") is a strategic, enterprise-wide 
initiative to replace the Company's legacy MPLS network infrastructure with a modern, software-defined, cloud-
native managed network and security platform. The Programme encompasses four primary workstreams 
executed in parallel under a unified programme governance structure:
• 
Workstream 1 — Managed SD-WAN Deployment: Full replacement of MPLS circuits across all 300 
branch and hub sites with a managed SD-WAN overlay, incorporating active-active dual-link 
configuration at Tier-1 hub sites.
• 
Workstream 2 — Cloud Direct Interconnect: Establishment of dedicated, carrier-grade private 
interconnects from Apex's Mumbai Data Centre directly to Microsoft Azure (ExpressRoute 
equivalent) and Amazon Web Services (AWS Direct Connect equivalent) at 10 Gbps each.
• 
Workstream 3 — Managed Security Operations Centre: Deployment and continuous 24x7x365 
monitoring of next-generation firewalls at all 300 branch edges by a certified SOC, including threat 
intelligence, SIEM correlation, and SLA-backed incident response.
• 
Workstream 4 — Enterprise IoT Mobility Platform: Consolidation of 2.5 million fleet and asset 
tracking SIMs onto a single enterprise mobility management platform with unified dashboard, eSIM 
capability, and enforced MDM policy.

3.3  Out of Scope 
The following items are explicitly excluded from the scope of this RFP. Vendors must not include pricing for 
out-of-scope items unless explicitly invited to do so in a separate commercial section:
• 
Passive civil or structural works at warehouse sites (cabling beyond network equipment patch 
panels, rack installations beyond those required for SD-WAN CPE).
• 
End-user computing (laptops, desktops, mobile handsets) not related to the IoT fleet tracking 
platform.
• 
SAP S/4HANA or WMS application management or licensing.

SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services
4.1  Workstream 1: Managed SD-WAN — All 300 Sites 
The vendor must design, deploy, and operate a fully managed Software-Defined Wide Area Network (SD-
WAN) across all 300 Apex branch warehouse and hub sites. The following specifications are mandatory:

4.1.2  SD-WAN Platform Mandatory Capabilities
• 
Policy-based intelligent traffic steering with Application-Aware Routing for classified applications 
including SAP S/4HANA, Microsoft Teams, Manhattan WMS, and IoT MQTT traffic.
• 
Dynamic Path Selection with real-time link quality measurement (latency, jitter, packet loss) with sub-
50 ms failover between primary and secondary links.
• 
Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP): All CPE devices must be provisioned remotely without on-site 
engineering resources. ZTP must enable a branch go-live within 4 hours of hardware delivery on-
site.
• 
Centralised management via a vendor-provided cloud-managed controller with full Apex read-only 
portal access, displaying per-site topology, link health, and traffic analytics.
• 
BGP and OSPF routing support with automated failover; static routing where dynamic protocols are 
not feasible at Tier-3 sites.
• 
Quality of Service (QoS) with a minimum of 8 traffic classes, including guaranteed bandwidth 
allocation for WMS and SAP traffic at all Tier-1 and Tier-2 sites.
• 
Integrated next-generation firewall (NGFW) capability at the SD-WAN edge CPE, including URL 
filtering, IPS/IDS, and SSL inspection, with policy managed from the SOC (Workstream 3).
• 
Support for 1:N VPN hub-and-spoke and any-to-any mesh topologies to enable direct branch-to-
branch connectivity for IoT data aggregation.
• 
Vendor must explicitly state the OEM hardware partner(s) proposed for SD-WAN CPE (e.g., Cisco 
Catalyst SD-WAN / Meraki, Fortinet FortiGate SD-WAN, VMware VeloCloud, Palo Alto Prisma SD-
WAN). Proposals specifying 'TBD' for hardware OEM will be deemed Non-Compliant.
4.2  Workstream 2: Cloud Direct Interconnects 
Apex requires the establishment of two (2) dedicated, private, carrier-grade Cloud Interconnects from its 
primary Mumbai Data Centre (NESCO IT Park, Goregaon East) as follows:
• 
Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute: Two (2) x 10 Gbps dedicated ExpressRoute circuits to the Microsoft 
Azure Central India region (pune1 / pune2 peering locations or equivalent Mumbai-region peering). 
Circuits must be configured in an active-active ECMP load-sharing arrangement with full BGP route 
redundancy. The interconnect must support both Azure Private Peering (for SAP and WMS 
workloads) and Microsoft Peering (for Microsoft 365).
• 
Amazon Web Services Direct Connect: One (1) x 10 Gbps Dedicated Connection to the AWS 
Mumbai (ap-south-1) region via a Direct Connect Location in Mumbai (STT GDC Hua Wei Navi

Mumbai or Netmagic Mumbai equivalent). The connection must support AWS Transit Gateway 
attachment for multi-VPC routing.
• 
The vendor must provide, operate, and manage the last-mile fibre and cross-connect from the Apex 
DC MMR to the respective carrier-neutral facility. Apex will provide DC rack space and power.
• 
Both interconnects must be monitored 24x7 by the vendor's NOC, with SLA-backed performance 
reporting including availability, latency, jitter, and throughput delivered monthly.
• 
The vendor must hold active AWS Direct Connect Partner and Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute 
Partner designations and must provide evidence of these designations with the proposal.
4.3  Workstream 3: 24/7 Managed Security Operations Centre (SOC) 
Apex requires a fully outsourced, 24x7x365 Managed SOC service to monitor, detect, investigate, and respond 
to cybersecurity threats across its entire network estate.
4.3.1  SOC Service Scope
• 
Continuous monitoring of all 300 SD-WAN edge NGFW devices for security events, policy violations, 
and anomalous traffic patterns.
• 
Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) — Vendor must operate a cloud-delivered 
SIEM platform (e.g., Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk, IBM QRadar, or equivalent) ingesting logs from all 
edge firewalls, the Mumbai DC, cloud interconnects, and endpoint detection agents.
• 
Threat Intelligence integration: Vendor must subscribe to at least two (2) commercial Threat 
Intelligence feeds (e.g., Recorded Future, CrowdStrike Intelligence, Mandiant) and correlate IOCs 
against Apex's network traffic in near-real-time.
• 
Vulnerability Management: Monthly automated VAPT scans across all in-scope network devices and 
quarterly manual penetration testing of the network perimeter, with findings reported to Apex CISO 
within 5 business days.
• 
Incident Response: The vendor must provide Tier-1, Tier-2, and Tier-3 analyst capability within the 
SOC, with defined escalation paths. P1 incidents must be acknowledged within 15 minutes and 
contained within 4 hours. P1 is defined as any confirmed active threat affecting more than 5 sites or 
the Mumbai DC.
• 
Root Cause Analysis (RCA): For all P1 and P2 security incidents, a formal RCA report must be 
delivered to Apex within 72 hours of incident containment, conforming to ITIL v4 Problem 
Management process.
• 
Monthly SOC Operations Report: Delivered by the 5th business day of the following month, 
containing: event volumes, MTTD, MTTR, top threats, false-positive rate, and open vulnerability 
status.
4.3.2  Data Residency for SOC
• 
All SIEM data, log archives, and security event records relating to Apex network traffic must be 
stored exclusively within India-based data centres (Tier III or above). Storage outside India is 
prohibited without explicit written authorisation from Apex CISO, consistent with DPDP Act 2023 
requirements.
• 
The vendor must provide the name, location, and third-party audit certification of the data centre(s) 
used for SIEM log storage as part of the proposal.
4.4  Workstream 4: Enterprise IoT and Mobility Connectivity 
Apex operates 2.5 million IoT SIMs across vehicle fleet trackers, warehouse asset tags, and cold-chain 
temperature sensors. The vendor must provide:
• 
A unified Enterprise Mobility Management (EMM) platform with a single dashboard enabling real-
time visibility of all 2.5 million SIMs, including active/inactive status, data consumption, location 
(where applicable), and MDM policy compliance.
• 
National Data Connectivity: Coverage across all 28 operational states with indoor and outdoor data 
coverage. The vendor must provide a coverage heatmap for all Tier-1 and Tier-2 site locations as 
part of the proposal.
• 
eSIM / eUICC Support: The platform must support eSIM remote provisioning for new device 
additions and SIM profile switching without physical SIM replacement.

Custom APN (cAPN): A dedicated private APN for Apex IoT SIM traffic, ensuring data does not 
traverse the public internet and is routed directly to Apex's network via private peering or MPLS 
handoff to the SD-WAN fabric.
• 
Data Plan Flexibility: Vendors must offer pooled data plans across SIM segments (fleet, assets, cold-

• 
API Integration: The EMM platform must expose RESTful APIs for integration with Apex's proprietary

SECTION 5 — Technical and Functional Requirements
5.1  Instructions for Completion 
This section contains the definitive technical and functional requirements for the Network Transformation 
Programme. Vendors MUST respond to EVERY requirement listed in the matrix below. The following response 
codes apply:
• 
Compliant (C): The vendor's proposed solution fully satisfies the stated requirement in its standard, 
generally available form without bespoke development, workarounds, or future roadmap 
commitments.
• 
Partially Compliant (PC): The proposed solution partially satisfies the requirement. The 'Vendor 
Remarks' column MUST contain a specific description of the gap, the proposed resolution, and the 
estimated timeline to achieve full compliance.
• 
Non-Compliant (NC): The proposed solution does not satisfy the requirement. The 'Vendor Remarks' 
column MUST contain a full explanation. Proposals with more than four (4) Non-Compliant 
responses to Mandatory requirements will be disqualified.
VENDOR INSTRUCTION: Do NOT alter, re-number, or re-sequence Requirement IDs. Do NOT 
modify the column structure of the requirements table. Responses must be entered exclusively in 
the 'Vendor Response' and 'Vendor Remarks' columns. Any modification to the table structure will 
render the proposal non-evaluable.

SECTION 10 — Pricing and Cost Proposal Requirements
10.1  Pricing Principles 
The following mandatory commercial conditions apply to all pricing submitted in response to this RFP. 
Proposals not conforming to these conditions will be deemed commercially non-compliant:
• 
Price Validity: All unit prices and commercial rates must remain firm, fixed, and binding for a 
minimum period of 180 calendar days from the Final Proposal Submission Deadline.
• 
Currency: All pricing must be denominated in Indian Rupees (INR). Pricing denominated in foreign 
currencies without an INR equivalent will not be accepted.
• 
Tax Exclusivity: All prices must be stated exclusive of GST. GST will be assessed separately at the 
applicable rate.
• 
Itemised Pricing: Bundled lump-sum prices are not acceptable. Each line item must be individually 
priced to the granularity specified in Section 10.2.
• 
OpEx Hardware Model: All SD-WAN CPE hardware, edge firewall hardware, and any other network 
equipment required for this programme must be provided under a Vendor-owned, OpEx (Hardware-
as-a-Service) model. Apex will not purchase or capitalise network hardware. Vendors proposing 
CapEx hardware models will be scored negatively on commercial evaluation.
• 
Most Favoured Nation: Apex requires a Most Favoured Nation (MFN) clause in the resulting 
contract, guaranteeing that pricing offered to Apex is no less favourable than pricing offered to any 
comparable Indian enterprise customer of the vendor.

SECTION 11 — Terms and Conditions / Contractual Requirements
The following contractual terms will govern the Master Services Agreement (MSA) resulting from this 
procurement. Vendors must explicitly acknowledge each clause in their proposal. Proposed deviations must 
be submitted in the Deviations Register (Appendix G) with full commercial and legal justification.
11.1  Contract Term 
The initial contract term is thirty-six (36) months from the date of contract execution, with two (2) optional 
renewal periods of twelve (12) months each, exercisable at Apex's sole discretion with sixty (60) days' written 
notice prior to the end of each contract term.
DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — LEGAL REVIEW REQUIRED: The 36-month minimum contract 
term is included deliberately to test vendor legal pushback. Vendors are expected to negotiate a 
break clause at month 18 and a 24-month initial term option. Vendors accepting 36 months 
without deviation may be disadvantaged in final evaluation, as inflexibility on contract term is 
considered a commercial risk by Apex. Vendors must address this in Appendix G.
11.2  Payment Terms 
Payment terms for all monthly recurring charges (MRC) and one-time non-recurring charges (NRC) shall be 
as follows:
• 
Monthly Recurring Charges: Payable within ninety (90) days of invoice date (Net-90 days from 
invoice). Invoices must be submitted on the first business day of each calendar month for the 
preceding month's services.
• 
One-Time Non-Recurring Charges: Payable within ninety (90) days of invoice date, subject to Apex's 
acceptance of the relevant programme milestone.
DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — CASH FLOW RISK: Net-90 payment terms are commercially 
aggressive and are included to test vendor risk assessment. Industry standard for managed 
services is Net-30 to Net-45. Vendors who accept Net-90 without a late payment interest clause 
risk significant cash flow exposure. Vendors must propose a deviation in Appendix G, specifying 
proposed standard payment terms and a late payment interest rate consistent with the MSME 
Development Act, 2006 (where applicable) or contractual interest at the RBI repo rate plus 2%.
11.3  Liability 
• 
General Liability Cap: The vendor's total aggregate liability under the MSA for any and all claims in 
any 12-month period shall not exceed the total charges paid by Apex to the vendor in the preceding 
twelve (12) months.
• 
Data Breach and Catastrophic Failure: Notwithstanding the general liability cap, in the event of: (a) a 
confirmed data breach attributable to the vendor's negligence or wilful misconduct resulting in the 
exfiltration of personal data of more than 10,000 Apex data subjects; or (b) a complete network 
outage affecting more than 50 Tier-2 or Tier-3 sites simultaneously for a period exceeding 8 
continuous hours; the vendor shall be subject to unlimited liability with no financial cap.
• 
Consequential Loss: Subject to the unlimited liability carve-out above, neither party shall be liable to 
the other for any indirect, special, or consequential losses.
DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — UNLIMITED LIABILITY: The 'unlimited liability' provision for data 
breaches and catastrophic outages is included deliberately to test vendor legal diligence. No 
commercially reasonable vendor should accept unlimited liability. Vendors must propose in 
Appendix G a negotiated super-cap (e.g., 3x annual contract value) with specific carve-outs for 
gross negligence and wilful misconduct, and cyber liability insurance coverage minimums 
consistent with SEBI CSCRF requirements.

Pain Point | Current Impact on Operations
Legacy MPLS Network | All 300 branch warehouses are connected via legacy Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS) circuits procured from a single incumbent carrier. Bandwidth tiers are rigid (2 Mbps to 10 Mbps per branch), costly to upgrade, and require 60–90-day lead times for any configuration change.
Chronic Network Outages | The Company experienced an average of 4.3 unplanned network outages per month per region in FY2024–25, each lasting an average of 47 minutes. These outages directly halt warehouse management system (WMS) operations, fleet tracking updates, and ERP transaction processing, resulting in an estimated revenue impact of INR 3.8 Crore per month.
Single-Link Dependency | Hub warehouse sites (Tier-1) currently operate on a single MPLS link with no active failover path. Loss of connectivity at a hub site cascades to 8–15 dependent spoke sites reliant on the hub for WMS access and IoT aggregation.
No Cloud On-Ramp | Apex is executing a cloud-first transformation programme (ERP on Microsoft Azure, data analytics on AWS). Currently, all cloud traffic is backhauled through the Mumbai Data Centre, introducing latency of 180–340 ms for branch-to-cloud workloads and consuming disproportionate Data Centre bandwidth.
Security Perimeter Gaps | Branch sites lack next-generation firewall (NGFW) protection. A significant ransomware incident in Q3 FY2024 resulted in operational downtime of 72 hours across 14 warehouse sites and a forensic investigation cost of INR 1.1 Crore. Apex currently has no 24/7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) capability.
IoT Connectivity Fragmentation | Apex operates a fleet of 2.5 million IoT-enabled tracking SIMs across its vehicle fleet and warehouse assets. SIM Management is currently distributed across three different Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs), resulting in fragmented visibility, inconsistent data plans, and inability to enforce unified MDM policies.

Head of Delivery (Project Manager) | PMP or PRINCE2 Practitioner certification mandatory; 7+ years managing large multi-site network rollouts. | Day-to-day delivery management; site migration scheduling; milestone tracking; RAID log management.

IoT / Mobility Lead | 5+ years IoT/EMM platform management; GSMA eSIM certification preferred; telco SIM management background. | IoT SIM platform migration; EMM policy design; private APN architecture; API integration.

Priority | Incident Type | Definition | Penalty
P1 | Critical Network Outage | Complete loss of SD-WAN connectivity affecting more than 5 Tier-1 or 10 Tier-2 sites simultaneously, or complete loss of both cloud interconnects simultaneously, for any period exceeding 30 minutes. | 5% of total monthly invoice value per each 15-minute period (or part thereof) of outage duration, applied against Total Contract Value.

P3 | Non-Critical Incident | Single site outage exceeding 4-hour MTTR, or SLA reporting delay exceeding 30 minutes from incident detection. | 0.5% of total monthly invoice value per incident.
